pr.title: Fix ftcolanim/ftmaterial split and fix string usages in ftmaterial
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Corrected the boundary between ftmaterial.c and ftcolanim.c by moving two ftCo sleep/death helper functions at 0x800BFD04 and 0x800BFD9C out of ftmaterial and into ftcolanim, renaming their symbols from ftMaterial_* to ftCo_*. The PR also cleaned ftmaterial string usage by replacing external/base-offset string references with direct strings and HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls, and updated splits, symbols, headers, includes, and call sites accordingly.
what_changed: Changed the ftmaterial text split end from 0x800BFE6C to 0x800BFD04 and the ftcolanim text split start from 0x800BFE6C to 0x800BFD04.
Moved the .sdata2 range 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 from ftmaterial.c to ftcolanim.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Renamed symbols ftMaterial_800BFD04 and ftMaterial_800BFD9C to ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Moved the implementations of the two sleep/death helper functions from ftmaterial.c into ftcolanim.c.
Updated call sites in fighter.c, ft_0D31.c, ft_0D4D.c, and ftcommon.c to call ftCo_800BFD04 or ftCo_800BFD9C instead of the old ftMaterial_* names.
Moved prototypes for the two functions from ftmaterial.h to ftcolanim.h.
Reduced the ftMObj data symbol size from 0x150 to 0x50 and introduced separate labels/string symbols for the remaining data that followed it.
Changed ftMaterial_800BF260 to initialize class info with direct string literals "sysdolphin_base_library" and "ft_mobj" and sizeof expressions instead of external string labels and hardcoded sizes.
Replaced OSReport plus __assert patterns in ftmaterial.c with HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls containing direct assertion strings.
smart_moves: The split fix was done holistically: text ranges, sdata2 ownership, symbol names, function definitions, declarations, includes, and every known call site were updated together.
Renaming the moved functions to ftCo_* aligns their namespace with their behavior and new file placement in ftcolanim rather than leaving misleading ftMaterial_* names.
Using direct string literals and HSD_ASSERTREPORT in ftmaterial.c avoids fragile pointer arithmetic such as base + offsets and makes assert/report intent explicit.
The PR separated ftMObj from adjacent data/string blobs in symbols.txt instead of treating the entire 0x150-byte region as one global object.
The moved ftCo_800BFD9C implementation brought its actual dependencies with it, including ftCo_800D4F24, Player_GetEntityAtIndex, and gm_80167320 includes.
decomp_lessons: When a split boundary is wrong, do not only rename symbols: update splits.txt, symbols.txt, headers, source placement, and all consumers so the project structure matches the linker map.
Functions named for a subsystem should live with the matching subsystem when evidence supports it; here sleep/death common-fighter helpers fit ftCo/ftcolanim better than ftmaterial.
Data ownership can reveal bad file splits: the .sdata2 range associated with these functions belonged with ftcolanim after the boundary moved.
Avoid overlarge data symbols when adjacent data is actually separate objects or strings; reducing ftMObj to sizeof(HSD_MObjInfo)-sized data clarified following labels.
Replacing label-offset string hacks with direct string literals can improve readability and expose the intended library/class/assert strings, but may slightly perturb matching if not fully tuned.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions use the ftCo_ prefix, as shown by ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C after the rename.
Material-specific functions remain under ftMaterial_*, such as ftMaterial_800BF260, ftMaterial_800BF2B8, ftMaterial_800BF534, ftMaterial_800BF6BC, and ftMaterial_800BFB4C.
The mobj class-info global is named ftMObj and declared as HSD_MObjInfo.
Unknown data/string labels adjacent to known objects were left as lbl_803C69D0, lbl_803C6A5C, lbl_803C6A74, lbl_803C6A98, and lbl_803C6AA8 rather than overnaming without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted split boundaries at exact function address 0x800BFD04 so ftCo_800BFD04 and ftCo_800BFD9C compile as part of ftcolanim.
Transferred the 0x804D8C20-0x804D8C28 .sdata2 range to the unit that now owns the code using it.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT(line, 0, string) for failed free-color-register checks instead of manually calling OSReport and __assert with string-label offsets.
Used sizeof(HSD_MObjInfo) and sizeof(HSD_MObj) in hsdInitClassInfo rather than hardcoded 0x50 and 0x20.
decomp.dev reported new 100% matches for ftcolanim ftCo_800BFD04 (+152 bytes), ftCo_800BFD9C (+208 bytes), ftmaterial .sdata (+16 bytes), and ftcolanim .sdata2 (+8 bytes).

The same report also noted expected broken old ftmaterial matches for the moved functions and small regressions in ftMaterial_800BF260, ftMaterial_800BF534, and ftMaterial_800BF6BC, so evidence suggests the PR prioritized correct ownership/splitting over preserving every prior per-function match.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

Automated decomp.dev feedback reported net linked-code/data improvements and four new matches, while also flagging five broken matches corresponding mostly to the old ftmaterial ownership of moved code.
searchable_terms: can't find free color ratio register
follow_up_queries: Check whether ftMaterial_800BF260, ftMaterial_800BF534, and ftMaterial_800BF6BC were later restored to full match after the string cleanup.
Search for other ftMaterial_* functions whose behavior or call sites indicate they should actually be ftCo_* or live in ftcolanim.
Audit neighboring data around 0x803C6980-0x803C6AD0 to replace lbl_* names with better names when more evidence appears.
Look for other files using OSReport plus __assert with base-offset strings that could be converted to HSD_ASSERTREPORT safely.
